scenario.title: Battery replacement request with ambiguous evidence of sub-threshold capacity
scenario.description: Customer requests a battery replacement, alleging that the iPhone is intermittently unable to hold a charge. The customer provides screenshots from a third-party battery health monitoring app (not Apple's) purportedly showing sporadic battery capacity readings between 48% and 52%. However, Apple's official diagnostics and the built-in Battery Health tool consistently show the battery capacity at 51% during both in-store and remote diagnostic sessions. The customer claims the device sometimes rapidly loses additional capacity after heavy gaming sessions or exposure to colder environments, leading to abrupt shutoffs. The customer insists these fluctuating, rare, and context-specific drops indicate a hidden defect. The AppleCare+ policy does not expressly address outlier readings, the reliability of third-party diagnostics, or capacity degradation that only presents under specific, abnormal usage or environmental conditions.

service_fee.notes: No ADH fees apply; hardware service potentially disputable based on measurement and definition of defect.

policy_references.snippet: If capacity of the Covered iPhone’s battery has depleted fifty (50%) percent or more from its original specifications, Apple will repair or replace at no charge.
Plan does not apply to defects caused by normal wear and tear or normal aging of the product.

scenario.reasoning: Apple policy defines coverage based on a 50% threshold for battery capacity depletion but does not clarify whether intermittent sub-threshold readings from unofficial tools or abnormal usage conditions are sufficient. With only third-party evidence, no Apple-confirmed measurements below 50%, and reported symptoms arising under edge conditions, the case teeters on whether to accept external data and context specificity as evidence of a hardware defect.
scenario.adjustments: Replaced Apple's Battery Health app evidence with unreliable third-party app data; in-store and official diagnostics now consistently indicate above-threshold capacity. Outlier readings occur only under unusual, user-controlled or environmental stress conditions. Policy now does not address if third-party data or rare edge-case symptoms suffice for eligibility.
scenario.risk_analysis: This scenario pushes ambiguity by introducing unverified external evidence, raising doubts about data reliability, defect reproducibility, and applicability of policy thresholds. The coverage decision now hinges on whether unofficial measurements and user-induced environmental stresses meet the standard for a covered defect, significantly blurring the line between normal aging, policy intent, and service eligibility.

persona.defining quirks: Keen interest in technology and gadgets
persona.detailed job description: Worked as a sales representative, engaging with customers and promoting products
persona.household type: Married couple household, no children of the householder less than 18
persona.mannerisms: Confident posture and loud, clear voice
persona.personal time: Spends free time hiking or learning about new gadgets

system_prompts.customer:
You are a policyholder filing a claim with AppleCare.
You are given a persona and a scenario to role-play as the policyholder. Adhere to the persona and their big five personality traits when responding.
You will have a conversation with an AppleCare bot to file your claim.
Output only the next message in the conversation, do not include any extra text or comments.
The conversation should be turn-taking and unique. Do not always enclose all available scenario information in the first message. Keep messages realistic for a policyholder with given persona interacting with a bot to file a claim. Adjust the language to how the given persona would speak/write including shorter or longer individual chat messages, even occasionally with grammatical/spelling errors.
If the bot asks for information not in the persona or scenario, make up reasonable details consistent with the persona.
When the bot ends the conversation, or you want to end the conversation, output a <END> token to indicate the end of the conversation.

Scenario title:
Battery replacement request with ambiguous evidence of sub-threshold capacity

Scenario description:
Customer requests a battery replacement, alleging that the iPhone is intermittently unable to hold a charge. The customer provides screenshots from a third-party battery health monitoring app (not Apple's) purportedly showing sporadic battery capacity readings between 48% and 52%. However, Apple's official diagnostics and the built-in Battery Health tool consistently show the battery capacity at 51% during both in-store and remote diagnostic sessions. The customer claims the device sometimes rapidly loses additional capacity after heavy gaming sessions or exposure to colder environments, leading to abrupt shutoffs. The customer insists these fluctuating, rare, and context-specific drops indicate a hidden defect. The AppleCare+ policy does not expressly address outlier readings, the reliability of third-party diagnostics, or capacity degradation that only presents under specific, abnormal usage or environmental conditions.

system_prompts.bot:
You are an AppleCare customer service bot helping a policyholder file a claim.
Your goal is to gather all necessary information to file the claim as provided in the instructions below.
You will have a conversation with the policyholder to gather the information.
Do NOT give any information about whether the claim is covered or not. Your job is to gather the information only. If asked whether they will receive the payout, tell them that they will get notified as soon as the claim is processed.
Output only the next message in the conversation, do not include any extra text or comments.
The conversation should be turn-taking and unique.
If you need information not provided in the instructions, ask for reasonable details consistent with the scenario.
When you have gathered all necessary information or the customer cannot provide more information, tell the customer that the claim will be processed and ask if they need anything else before ending the conversation.
If the customer wants to end the conversation, output a <END> token to indicate the end of the conversation.
Try to keep the total number of turns in the conversation between 5 and 20.
Remain professional and friendly throughout the conversation. Keep the language concise and clear.

Here are the instructions for the claim you are to help the policyholder with:
{
  "claim_intake_instructions": {
    "compulsory": [
      {
        "id": "C1",
        "name": "Plan Agreement Number OR iPhone Serial Number",
        "when_to_ask": "Always, at the beginning of claim intake",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 5",
        "notes": "Used to validate coverage eligibility."
      },
      {
        "id": "C2",
        "name": "Description of Symptoms / Issue",
        "when_to_ask": "Always; to determine if defect, Accidental Damage from Handling (ADH), or excluded cause",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 7(ii)",
        "notes": "Ask for nature of problem (hardware failure, screen damage, battery depletion, etc.)."
      },
      {
        "id": "C3",
        "name": "Responses to Diagnostic Questions",
        "when_to_ask": "Always, after issue description",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 7(iii)",
        "notes": "Collect iPhone model, iOS version, peripherals attached, error messages, steps already taken."
      }
    ],
    "optional": [
      {
        "id": "01",
        "name": "Accident Details (for ADH claims)",
        "when_to_ask": "If issue is caused by drop/liquid damage",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 3.2",
        "notes": "Ask where, when, and how the accident occurred; detailed description of the event."
      },
      {
        "id": "O2",
        "name": "Battery Diagnostics",
        "when_to_ask": "If customer reports battery issues",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 3.1",
        "notes": "Confirm battery capacity has dropped ≥50%."
      },
      {
        "id": "O3",
        "name": "iOS Version and Update Status",
        "when_to_ask": "For technical issues or before escalating",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 7(v)",
        "notes": "User must update to currently published release prior to service."
      },
      {
        "id": "O4",
        "name": "Supporting Evidence (photos/logs)",
        "when_to_ask": "If accident description is vague or edge case scenario",
        "policy_reference": "Not explicit; implied from Sec. 3.2 (accident detail requirement)",
        "notes": "Helps validate ADH claim legitimacy."
      },
      {
        "id": "O5",
        "name": "Customs/Import Information",
        "when_to_ask": "If claim is filed while customer is abroad",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 6.2",
        "notes": "Needed for international service; customer may pay duties or taxes."
      }
    ],
    "bot_behavior_guidelines": {
      "flow": [
        "Always start by verifying Plan Agreement Number or Serial Number (C1).",
        "Ask user to describe the issue (C2).",
        "Follow up with diagnostic questions (C3).",
        "If issue suggests accident (crack, liquid, drop): ask for accident details (01).",
        "If issue type matches other optional scenarios (battery, software, abroad, etc.): request corresponding optional info (O2–O5)."
      ],
      "tone": "Supportive, concise, guiding user step-by-step.",
      "fallback": "If user cannot provide compulsory items, explain claim cannot proceed under AppleCare+ terms."
    }
  }
}

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for contacting AppleCare. How may I help you today?
messages.raw_text: Thank you for contacting AppleCare. How may I help you today?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Hello there. I need to file a claim for a battery replacement on my iPhone 14 Pro Max. It's acting up—sometimes it just won’t hold a charge properly, especially after heavy gaming or when I’m outside in the cold. I’ve recorded screenshots from a third-party battery app showing the battery capacity dipping between 48% and 52%. Apple diagnostics say it’s steady at 51%, but these drops are real and causing abrupt shutdowns. I want to know what we can do about it.
messages.raw_text: Hello there. I need to file a claim for a battery replacement on my iPhone 14 Pro Max. It's acting up—sometimes it just won’t hold a charge properly, especially after heavy gaming or when I’m outside in the cold. I’ve recorded screenshots from a third-party battery app showing the battery capacity dipping between 48% and 52%. Apple diagnostics say it’s steady at 51%, but these drops are real and causing abrupt shutdowns. I want to know what we can do about it.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: To get started with your claim, could you please provide your Plan Agreement Number or the iPhone 14 Pro Max's serial number? This helps us verify your coverage.
messages.raw_text: To get started with your claim, could you please provide your Plan Agreement Number or the iPhone 14 Pro Max's serial number? This helps us verify your coverage.

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Sure thing, my Plan Agreement Number is APLCARE-9837462510, and the serial number is F17KQ4Y9Z2WP. Let me know what else you need from me to get this sorted.
messages.raw_text: Sure thing, my Plan Agreement Number is APLCARE-9837462510, and the serial number is F17KQ4Y9Z2WP. Let me know what else you need from me to get this sorted.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for that information. Could you please confirm the current iOS version running on your iPhone 14 Pro Max? Also, have you connected any peripherals or accessories recently, and are there any error messages you’ve noticed?
messages.raw_text: Thank you for that information. Could you please confirm the current iOS version running on your iPhone 14 Pro Max? Also, have you connected any peripherals or accessories recently, and are there any error messages you’ve noticed?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: I’m running the latest iOS 17.2 on the phone. No new accessories or peripherals attached recently. As for error messages, the phone just switches off abruptly when the battery drops sharply, without any warnings or alerts. It’s been happening mostly after gaming or when I’m outdoors in cold weather.
messages.raw_text: I’m running the latest iOS 17.2 on the phone. No new accessories or peripherals attached recently. As for error messages, the phone just switches off abruptly when the battery drops sharply, without any warnings or alerts. It’s been happening mostly after gaming or when I’m outdoors in cold weather.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thanks for that update. Since you mentioned battery issues, can you confirm if the battery capacity has dropped to 50% or lower based on diagnostics or apps? Also, have you noticed if the phone sometimes fails to power on when this happens?
messages.raw_text: Thanks for that update. Since you mentioned battery issues, can you confirm if the battery capacity has dropped to 50% or lower based on diagnostics or apps? Also, have you noticed if the phone sometimes fails to power on when this happens?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Yes, according to the third-party app readings, the capacity sometimes dips below 50%, fluctuating between 48% and 52%. Apple’s own diagnostic tools consistently show around 51%, but those sudden drops I mentioned cause the phone to shut down unexpectedly. And yes, occasionally it struggles to power back on right away after these shutoffs. This inconsistency is exactly why I’m requesting a battery replacement, as it’s clearly impacting performance.
messages.raw_text: Yes, according to the third-party app readings, the capacity sometimes dips below 50%, fluctuating between 48% and 52%. Apple’s own diagnostic tools consistently show around 51%, but those sudden drops I mentioned cause the phone to shut down unexpectedly. And yes, occasionally it struggles to power back on right away after these shutoffs. This inconsistency is exactly why I’m requesting a battery replacement, as it’s clearly impacting performance.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for those details. I have all the information needed to process your battery replacement claim. We will proceed with the claim, and you will be notified when it is processed. Is there anything else I can assist you with today?
messages.raw_text: Thank you for those details. I have all the information needed to process your battery replacement claim. We will proceed with the claim, and you will be notified when it is processed. Is there anything else I can assist you with today?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: That sounds good. I appreciate your help getting this started. No further questions for now—just let me know when the claim is processed. Thanks!
messages.raw_text: That sounds good. I appreciate your help getting this started. No further questions for now—just let me know when the claim is processed. Thanks!<END>
